stepsShipped.deliverable: Foundation migration for Cluster B (M9 Org Chart, audit row 2.16, P0). Restores org_edges table with two design upgrades over the worktree-phase-2-the-org reference SQL: (1) ON DELETE CASCADE on every FK (worktree had bare FKs), keeps hard-deletes safe when M-C step e ships companies.delete; (2) composite (company_id, manager_id) index for orgchart.get tree projection (M-C step c). UNIQUE on report_id enforces single-manager-per-report at SQL layer; cycle prevention deferred to step c repo layer (wouldCycle helper). Hand-authored migration SQL because drizzle-kit auto-emit dumps every table — repo skips snapshots after 0002 by long-standing convention (only 0000/0001/0002 snapshots exist in meta/). Drizzle-kit's emitted snapshot was deleted to match the convention.

stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores Cluster A multi-company CRUD foundation per audit row 10.12 (P0; Rocky's locked M7 architectural decision — NOT aspirational). The companies.create IPC channel ships with handler + preload bridge + register wiring + 28 unit tests, atomically seeding both system pseudo-employees (system-agent from M31 + system-copilot from M33) before returning so every new company is fully usable on first observation. Mirrors the seed-flow + M33 F4 post-restore invariant. Input validation: non-empty trimmed name (≤120 chars), slug regex /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ (URL-safe; matches existing 'strategia-x' convention), settings shape guard, icon/theme type guards. Duplicate slug surfaces SQL UNIQUE constraint as friendlier 'slug … is already in use' message. Missing ensureSystemForCompany dep throws (refuses to leave a half-bootstrap row); bus-emit failure is logged + swallowed (durable write already landed). New 'company.created' bus event satisfies architectural invariant #11.

designDecisions.depShape: ensureSystemForCompany?: (companyId) => { agentEmployeeId, copilotEmployeeId, agentCreated, copilotCreated } — optional on the deps interface (matches the M33 F4 ensurePostRestoreBootstrap convention) but the handler throws when undefined because companies.create cannot ship a usable company without the system pair. Composition root captures db + roleLoader in the closure, same handles the F4 sweep uses.
designDecisions.slugRegex: /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ — lowercase alphanumerics + hyphen, 1–63 chars, no leading hyphen. Matches the existing strategia-x slug + the M-D CreateCompanyDialog will hand-roll the same regex. Enforced at IPC boundary so SQL UNIQUE failures surface only for genuine duplicates.
designDecisions.duplicateSlugMessage: Repo throws raw 'UNIQUE constraint failed: companies.slug' on duplicate. Handler catches + rewraps as 'slug … is already in use' for renderer toast UX while preserving any other repo throw verbatim.
designDecisions.busEmitTolerance: company.created bus emit failure does NOT cascade into IPC throw. The repo write + bootstrap already succeeded; bus failure is logged + the response still includes the new ids. Mirrors companies.archive's invariant-#11 pattern.
designDecisions.missingDepThrow: ensureSystemForCompany unwired throws BEFORE any SQL writes. Different from M33 F4 (which warns + degrades on a missing bootstrap because the DB + vault are already swapped) — companies.create is a fresh-state operation, refusing to ship a half-formed company is correct fail-loud behavior.
designDecisions.responseShape: Returns { companyId, systemAgentEmployeeId, systemCopilotEmployeeId } so the renderer can open Copilot Conversations or the command palette without a follow-up employees.list round-trip (those rows are filtered out of listVisibleByCompany by the is_system filter sweep).
designDecisions.newBusEvent: company.created added to EventType union + CompanyCreatedPayload interface added. The events.ts comment block at line 425+ explicitly anticipated this — 'No company.created twin today — the design-doc decision log keeps creation as an IPC-only side effect until companies.create lands.' Phase 5.6 M-C step b is when companies.create lands.

stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores Cluster B (M9 org chart) backend READ surface per audit rows 2.16 / 2.21. Builds on step (a) Migration 0013 (org_edges table) and step (b) companies.create (system-employee bootstrap). Ships the full org-edges repo factory + narrow IPC interface + orgchart.get handler + preload + composition-root wiring in one atomic. Response shape is flat {employees[], edges[], rootIds[]} — renderer builds whatever tree layout (indented list, tree view, Sankey) O(n) from parallel arrays without a follow-up IPC round-trip. Defensive edge filter at handler time drops edges referencing employees outside listVisibleByCompany (system pseudo-employees, soft/hard-deleted rows, or cross-company FK violations from direct-DB writes).

designDecisions.responseShape: Flat parallel arrays {employees[], edges[], rootIds[]}. Renderer builds tree O(n) keyed by managerId. Decision: don't pre-nest on the server — the renderer may want multiple layouts (indented list, tree view, Sankey, reports-to cards) and serving flat data lets it choose without a follow-up IPC.
designDecisions.defensiveEdgeFilter: Edges whose managerId OR reportId references an employee outside listVisibleByCompany are DROPPED at handler time (not repo layer). Catches: (1) system pseudo-employees that a direct-DB-write incorrectly pointed at; (2) soft/hard-deleted employees whose edges have not been cleaned up by a future employees.fire flow; (3) cross-company FK violations from a drizzle-studio direct edit. The repo's wouldCycle is the write-path canonical guard; this read filter is belt-and-suspenders.
designDecisions.narrowInterface: IpcOrgEdgesRepo exposes only listByCompany to the IPC layer. setManager/wouldCycle/removeByReport/removeByManager are declared on createOrgEdgesRepo but NOT in IpcOrgEdgesRepo — they land in the IPC surface when step d's employees.setManager IPC ships. Keeps the IPC-facing interface tight so unit-test fakes don't have to implement the full repo surface until the write-side IPCs need it.
designDecisions.rootIdsConvenience: rootIds surfaced as a first-class response field rather than computed client-side. Renderer reads it directly to find tree roots (typically the single CEO row) without iterating edges. Also useful for empty-state detection — rootIds === [] + employees === [] means a fresh company with no org wired yet.
designDecisions.noBusEmit: orgchart.get is read-only; invariant #11 (IPC mutations must emit bus events) does not apply. No new EventType required in this step.

stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores Cluster B (M9 org chart) write-side surface per audit rows 2.19 + 2.20. Builds on step (c) org-edges repo (setManager + removeByReport + wouldCycle + getByReport already wired); step (d) ships the IPC layer + bus events per architectural invariant #11. employees.promote performs an atomic role swap (roleId / level / title / roleMdSha / tools_*_json updated in place; pure SQL — does NOT touch org-edges) and emits employee.promoted with full pre/post snapshot for renderer delta rendering. employees.setManager handles both upsert (managerId !== null → orgEdgesRepo.setManager with pre-flight wouldCycle rejection) and detach (managerId === null → removeByReport, report becomes graph root) paths and emits employee.managerSet with previousManagerId snapshot for renderer animation. Both handlers refuse framework-internal employees on every relevant side (employees.fire defense-in-depth) and reject cross-company edges. Composition root requires no edit — createOrgEdgesRepo(db) already returns the full repo surface that satisfies the widened IpcOrgEdgesRepo interface.

designDecisions.promotePreservesName: Promote is a role change, not a rename — the row's `name` column is intentionally untouched. A future employees.update / rename channel handles the rename case separately. Mirrors the seed-flow + the existing updateStatus convention (single-responsibility writes).
designDecisions.setManagerNullDetach: managerId === null is the documented detach path: handler dispatches orgEdgesRepo.removeByReport(employeeId) and the report becomes a graph root. managerId === undefined OR empty-string is rejected with `must be a non-empty string or null (to detach)` so callers never silently no-op when they meant to either set or clear.
designDecisions.preFlightCycleCheck: The repo's setManager runs wouldCycle internally and throws a raw 'would create cycle' error. The IPC handler runs the same check BEFORE invoking the repo so the renderer toast surfaces a friendlier 'would create reporting cycle — managerId already reports (directly or transitively) to employeeId' message. Belt-and-suspenders: the inner repo guard remains so a non-IPC caller (drizzle-studio direct write, future CLI) cannot bypass it.
designDecisions.promotedAtVsSetAt: EmployeePromotedPayload uses promotedAt; EmployeeManagerSetPayload uses setAt. Distinct field names so a payload-shape diff in audit-view chips immediately tells which event landed. Both are wall-clock ms timestamps captured AFTER the durable write succeeds.
designDecisions.previousManagerIdSnapshot: setManager handler reads orgEdgesRepo.getByReport(employeeId) BEFORE the repo write so the bus event payload carries `previousManagerId` for renderer animation. Snapshot read is O(1) under the UNIQUE index on report_id; the inner setManager re-reads the same row but the cost is negligible compared to a follow-up renderer round-trip to compute the delta.
designDecisions.systemEmployeeDefenseInDepth: Both handlers refuse framework-internal employees (is_system: true) on every relevant side — promote refuses promoting a system row, setManager refuses both report=system AND manager=system. Mirrors employees.fire's last-line-of-defense check. The hire dialog already filters via listVisibleByCompany, but a direct IPC call bypasses the renderer entirely.
designDecisions.widenedIpcOrgEdgesRepo: IpcOrgEdgesRepo gained four methods (getByReport / setManager / removeByReport / wouldCycle) — the write-side surface step c declared as 'lands in step d' inline JSDoc. Existing FakeOrgEdgesRepo in orgchart-handlers.test.ts gains stub methods that throw 'unused by orgchart.get tests' so a future cross-test accidentally exercising them fails loud rather than silently no-oping. New step d test files hand-roll their own focused fakes per the orgchart-handlers.test.ts convention.

S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff. S4 verifiedBy populated on the paired ledger entry per contract. M-E acceptance #ciGate continues to validate.

Allowlist shrinks 5 → 3 (the remaining 3 entries are mcp.* naming-drift rows that land in M-F as docs-only IPC table renames). companies.delete is the destructive sibling of companies.archive — the handler MUST quiesce copilot analyzer + event window the same way archive does, then hard-delete (CASCADE FK on org_edges from migration 0013 cleans up the reporting graph). New bus events: company.updated + company.deleted per invariant #11. After step (e): M-C complete, head-of-queue advances to M-D (UI Backfill).

stepsShipped.deliverable: Hardens step (d) IPC surface to PRODUCTION-READY for M-D wiring. Eight QA findings (BUG-001 through BUG-008) + six coverage gaps addressed in one atomic commit per Rocky's iron rule (zero deferring). P2 fixes (level-inversion guard + archived-company guard) prevent the most likely M-D demo regressions; P3 fixes (atomic transactions, actorId consistency, cache-staleness contract pin, trust-boundary docs) close the durable design contracts so step (d) IPCs cannot silently drift in future refactors. The atomic transaction refactor on org-edges setManager + removeByReport eliminates the TOCTOU window that previously made cycle checks racy under concurrent IPC traffic; the snapshot read inside the transaction guarantees the bus event payload's previousManagerId reflects the exact state the upsert overwrote. The level-inversion guard locks the Phase 2 M9 hierarchy at the IPC boundary with a normalized lookup that handles role-pack frontmatter shape variation. The assertCompanyActive helper is reusable for step (e) companies.update.

designDecisions.levelRankFailOpen: getLevelRank returns null for unknown level strings; the IPC handler treats null as 'skip the inversion check + log a dev-mode warning'. Strict-reject would brick the IPC if a future role-pack adds a new tier the rank table doesn't know about. Fail-open + warning surfaces the operational signal without blocking work — cleaner trade-off than a fragile strict check.
designDecisions.strictRankRule: rank(manager) MUST be strictly less than rank(report). Same-level relationships are rejected (no peer-as-manager); inverted relationships are rejected. Matrix / flat-org structures would require an explicit opt-in flag in a future milestone — today the strict rule reflects the locked Phase 2 M9 'real hierarchy' design intent.
designDecisions.atomicSetManagerSignature: setManager / removeByReport signatures changed to return previousManagerId snapshots from inside the transaction. The breaking signature change updates 3 test files in lockstep — preferred over an additive `setManagerAtomic` second method because the additive route would let future callers accidentally use the non-atomic version. Single-source-of-truth atomicity.
designDecisions.handlerCycleRewrap: Handler-side wouldCycle pre-check REMOVED (eliminated TOCTOU). The repo's setManager runs the cycle check inside its own transaction. Handler catches the repo throw and pattern-matches the `[org-edges] setManager: would create cycle` prefix to surface a friendlier renderer-facing 'would create reporting cycle' message. Non-cycle errors pass through unmodified — defense in depth verified by the new 'preserves non-cycle repo errors without rewrap' test.
designDecisions.assertCompanyActiveScope: Helper refuses both 'archived' status AND missing companies — paranoid by design. Step (e) companies.update will reuse the same helper. The error message includes the originating IPC channel name so debugging across multiple write-paths stays unambiguous.
designDecisions.actorIdSweepScope: BUG-005 sweep limited to bus-emit actorId in step (d) handlers + companies.create + companies.archive (the upstream sibling handlers that drift the same way). NOT applied to command-service.ts's `actorId: req.actorId?.trim() || 'user'` fallback because that 'user' literal is a renderer-side discriminator string for the human-actor label in commands-view.tsx — changing it would break the renderer contract. Scope decision documented to prevent over-reach in future sweeps.
designDecisions.preExistingLintSuppression: entity-resolver.ts non-null-assertion errors were pre-existing M30 NLU code; biome's diagnostic-threshold-truncation hid them in earlier verification gates. The hardening pass surfaced them as the lint baseline tightened. Three narrowly-scoped biome-ignore comments justified in-place by prior length checks; addressed inline per iron rule rather than deferred.
designDecisions.noE2EReRun: Step (d) hardening adds no new IPC channels and no new renderer contract — only behavioral guarantees on existing channels (atomic transactions + new validation throws). The existing E2E baseline at step c (11 specs / 12 cases) is preserved unchanged. M-D will land the renderer surface E2E.

stepsShipped.safeguardProof: Fifth M-C proof at scale of the M-E safeguards. S2 conformance gate ran GREEN on c6b118a — no allowlist movement expected (no new IPCs added in hardening). S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff. S4 verifiedBy populated on the paired ledger entry per contract. The hardening commit demonstrates the safeguard works for the symmetric case (changes that don't touch claimable surface) just as well as the additive case (M-C steps a/b/c/d that did).
stepsShipped.nextStep: step (e) — companies.update + companies.delete IPCs (Cluster A finalization per audit rows 10.13 + 10.15). Both currently on allowlist; restoration shrinks 5 → 3 (mcp.* naming-drift rows remain for M-F docs sweep). companies.delete must mirror companies.archive's quiesce (analyzer.stop + eventWindow.clear) BEFORE the hard delete; CASCADE FK on org_edges from migration 0013 cleans the reporting graph. The new assertCompanyActive helper from BUG-002 hardening will be reused by companies.update for the 'cannot update an archived company' guard. New bus events: company.updated + company.deleted per invariant #11. After step (e) ships: M-C complete, head-of-queue advances to M-D (UI Backfill).

stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores Cluster A multi-company CRUD write-side surface per audit rows 10.13 + 10.15. Completes the Cluster A restoration (step b seeded companies.create; step e finalizes update + delete). companies.update performs a single UPDATE with only supplied patch fields (name/slug/settings/icon/theme; icon accepts null to clear) behind the assertCompanyActive guard introduced in step d hardening. companies.delete is the destructive sibling of companies.archive — hard-deletes the company row AND every company-scoped row across 15 tables in a single transaction with FK-safe order (indirect leaves → cross-referencing children [meetings/tickets/projects] → direct children [threads/goals/file_vault/embeddings/command_history] → loose-reference rows [mcp_servers company-scoped only; NULL=global survives; events] → employees [CASCADEs org_edges via migration 0013] → copilot_insights [CASCADE via schema] → companies). The quiesce pipeline (analyzer.stop → eventWindow.clear) runs BEFORE the transactional sweep so a mid-tick analyzer cannot observe rows about to disappear — identical contract to companies.archive. Pre-transaction snapshot read captures name+slug so the company.deleted bus event payload carries a valid identifier after the row is gone. M-C is now complete.

designDecisions.hardDeleteScope: Rocky-approved hard-delete (plan text over allowlist 'soft-delete' phrasing, confirmed mid-session). The hard-delete semantic made sense because 'destructive sibling of companies.archive' has no meaning if both are soft-deletes. Implementation: explicit 15-table transactional cleanup in FK-safe order rather than schema migration adding CASCADE to 9 non-cascading FKs (migration would require table-recreate per FK under SQLite ALTER-TABLE constraints — far bigger scope than the restoration target).
designDecisions.fkSafeOrder: Three-phase deletion ordered by FK-topology not table name. Phase 1 (indirect leaves): thread_members/messages/runs point at threads; project_tickets/ticket_attachments point at projects/tickets. Phase 2 (cross-referencing): meetings.threadId + chairId and tickets.threadId + assigneeId and projects.goalId + leadId all point at later-deleted tables — they MUST drop before threads/goals/employees. Phase 3 (direct company children): threads/goals/file_vault/embeddings/command_history. Phase 4 (loose): mcp_servers company-scoped only (NULL=global survives per 'only scoped rows get swept' contract) + events (no FK, cleaned for hygiene). Phase 5: employees cascades org_edges automatically. Phase 6: copilot_insights cascades via schema but explicit DELETE stays for audit-log parity. Phase 7: the target company row.
designDecisions.transactionalAtomicity: db.transaction(tx => ...) wraps the whole sweep. A mid-sweep throw rolls every DELETE back — callers never see a half-deleted company. The repo test harness proves this against real sql.js with foreign_keys=ON by seeding rows across all 15 tables for BOTH the target company AND a sibling, then asserting after delete that (a) every scoped row for the target is gone, (b) every corresponding row for the sibling survives, (c) the NULL-companyId global mcp_server survives, (d) indirect leaves (thread_members/messages/runs/project_tickets/ticket_attachments) mirror the same surgical pattern.
designDecisions.archivedIsDeletable: companies.delete EXPLICITLY allows deleting archived companies — delete is 'permanently remove' (user-intent), archive is 'soft-hide' (orchestrator-pause). Deleting an already-archived row is valid. Only GENUINELY-missing ids throw 'company not found' so a caller with a stale reference knows to refresh rather than silent-no-op.
designDecisions.snapshotBeforeDelete: The delete handler reads companiesRepo.getById(id) BEFORE the transactional sweep so the bus event payload carries slug + name + deletedAt after the row is gone. Without the snapshot, subscribers would need to look up the row post-delete and find nothing. Same pattern as employees.setManager's previousManagerId capture from step d.
designDecisions.updateAssertActive: companies.update reuses the assertCompanyActive helper introduced in step d hardening (BUG-002). Archived companies cannot be mutated back to a live-looking row without an explicit reactivation path (future milestone). Helper throws on missing + archived + unknown ids with a clear 'reactivate before mutating org' message.
designDecisions.updateEmptyPatchEmits: An empty patch is allowed at both the repo and handler layers. Repo short-circuits without issuing SQL; handler still emits company.updated with empty patchedKeys. Rationale: optimistic update paths in the M-D renderer may fire a no-op update to reconcile timestamp state and a missing bus event would leave them stuck. The emit is the invariant #11 anchor, not the SQL write.
designDecisions.patchedKeysPayload: company.updated payload carries patchedKeys (typed subset) rather than the full patch values. Audit-view chips and renderer caches only need the keys to render a delta chip and invalidate the right query slices. Omitting values keeps the bus event small AND prevents sensitive settings fragments from landing on an append-only log.
designDecisions.iconNullAccepted: icon accepts null (to clear) in CompaniesUpdateRequest + the repo patch shape. The DB column is nullable so null propagates through drizzle to the SQL UPDATE. theme does NOT have an equivalent 'clear' path because the domain has no meaningful empty state — a future milestone can widen if needed.
designDecisions.duplicateSlugRewrap: Both companies.create and companies.update rewrap SQL UNIQUE violations on slug as 'slug "X" is already in use' — same friendlier message shape for consistency. Non-UNIQUE repo throws pass through verbatim (disk full, permission denied, etc.) so schema-drift failures surface with raw sqlite text for diagnosis.
designDecisions.repoVsHandlerBoundary: Bus emit lives on the handler. The repo stays free of event-bus imports to preserve the architectural boundary archive() already observes (step b convention). Composition root captures the bus once and injects through IpcHandlerDeps.
designDecisions.fakeWideningPattern: Four existing test files (handlers.test.ts + companies-handlers.test.ts + employees-promote-handlers.test.ts + employees-set-manager-handlers.test.ts) gain stub update + delete methods on their FakeCompaniesRepo that throw 'unused' — same belt-and-suspenders pattern step d used for the FakeOrgEdgesRepo in orgchart-handlers.test.ts. A future cross-test accidentally reaching those fakes fails loud rather than silently no-oping.

stepsShipped.safeguardProof: Sixth M-C proof at scale of the M-E safeguards. S2 conformance gate ran GREEN on fd3617b — both new IPC allowlist entries deleted in the same atomic so audit:claims 90/5/0 → 92/3/0 (the new IPCs are verified-on-disk, not allowlisted). S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff. S4 verifiedBy populated on this paired ledger entry per contract. M-E acceptance #ciGate continues to validate — five additive commits (a/b/c/d/e) + one hardening commit all with clean safeguards throughout the entire M-C run.
stepsShipped.nextStep: M-C COMPLETE. Head-of-queue advances to M-D (UI Backfill). M-D ships the renderer surface for Cluster A multi-company CRUD (WorkspaceSwitcher + CreateCompanyDialog + CompanySettings panel wired to companies.create/update/archive/delete) + Cluster B org chart (indented-list tree UI + drag-to-rearrange wired to orgchart.get + employees.promote + employees.setManager) + invariant #11 bus event subscriptions for cache invalidation + E2E specs for user-facing surface. M-D's DoR is this ledger commit closing M-C's exit-KPI. Allowlist preserved at 3 entries (all mcp.* naming-drift rows scheduled for M-F docs sweep); M-D cannot shrink the allowlist further.

stepsShipped.deliverable: Closes the FOLLOWUP-P1 main-side Invariant #11 gap surfaced by the 2026-04-18 ground-zero QA audit §3.1. Prior to step f, 14 state-mutating tickets.*/projects.*/goals.* IPC handlers completed their durable writes without emitting bus events, so cross-process mutations (E2E specs, orchestrator, agent tools not routed through task.* planner events) left renderer React Query caches stale. Step f ships 14 new EventType union members + 14 payload interfaces + 14 handler emits + 3 renderer hook widenings + 37 unit tests in a single atomic commit. The architectural invariant #11 contract is now complete on both sides — every ticket/project/goal/company/employee mutation IPC emits a bus event, every renderer cache subscribes via its useXxxEventSync hook. Scope-aligned with Rocky's ~1000 LOC estimate (actual atomic diff: +1800/-40 including tests + plan doc). No new IPC channels, no repo interface changes, no E2E contract break — purely additive event emission + renderer subscription widening. Attachment lifecycle (tickets.attachFile/detachFile) is deferred to a later FOLLOWUP-P1-extended milestone before M-D's attachment UI needs cache invalidation.

designDecisions.scope14Events: Clean 14-event envelope matches Rocky's scoping note (6 ticket + 5 project + 3 goal). tickets.attachFile + tickets.detachFile scoped OUT of step f to preserve the 14-event clean count; they ship as a later FOLLOWUP-P1-extended milestone before M-D's attachment UI lands. Rationale: keeps the step f atomic reviewable + preserves a clean follow-up card on the attachments domain.
designDecisions.emitPatternMirrorsCompaniesCreate: Every new emit uses the exact companies.create / companies.archive pattern — if (bus) { try { bus.emit(...) } catch (err) { console.error('[ipc] <channel>: bus emit failed (row still <verb>):', err) } } else if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') { console.warn('bus dep unwired — renderer caches will NOT invalidate') }. Inline vs extracted helper decision: inline. Existing 6 emits already inline; introducing a helper would require refactoring them in lockstep to avoid inconsistency, which CLAUDE.md 'don't refactor beyond the task' explicitly discourages.
designDecisions.humanUserIdActor: All 14 emits use actorId: HUMAN_USER_ID + actorKind: 'user' per the BUG-005 sweep convention shipped in M-C step d hardening. Agent-initiated mutations (M32 delegate_subtask → tickets.create) today emit through their own task.delegated event and do NOT round-trip through tickets.create — so the 'always HUMAN_USER_ID' rule is correct for current callers. If a future milestone threads agent actor context through the ticket IPC surface, the actor plumbing lands in that milestone, not here.
designDecisions.snapshotBeforeDrop: goal.deleted, project.deleted, and project.ticketUnlinked handlers capture needed fields (title, companyId) BEFORE the repo write so the bus payload carries valid identifiers after the row is gone. Mirrors company.deleted (step e) + employee.managerSet (step d) pattern. project.ticketUnlinked captures companyId from the pre-unlink project fetch since ticketId is already in the request.
designDecisions.ticketsCreateDualEmit: ticketsCreate fires ticket.created FIRST (always), then ticket.assigned SECOND (only when the immediate-assign branch runs AND the employee + ticket lookups both succeed). The previousAssigneeId on the ticket.assigned payload is null by definition in this path since the ticket was created with the assigneeId — no prior assignee existed. This matches how M-D's WorkspaceSwitcher/TicketsView UX flows will consume the events (create-then-reassign vs create-already-assigned).
designDecisions.ticketsAssignPreviousSnapshot: ticketsAssign captures ticket.assigneeId into a local const BEFORE calling ticketsRepo.assign(). Must happen before the write — after the repo write, ticket.assigneeId still reflects the fetched (pre-assign) row because the fake + real repos mutate in place, but relying on that is fragile. Snapshot-then-write keeps the contract explicit + the test for reassign scenarios passes unambiguously.
designDecisions.projectsLinkTicketFetchGuard: projectsLinkTicket + projectsUnlinkTicket gained a projectsRepo.getById call at the top of the handler. Two benefits: (1) threads companyId through to the bus payload (required by the invariant #11 DashboardEvent envelope), (2) becomes a phantom-projectId validation guard so callers fail loud rather than silently writing to a non-existent project. Behavior improvement scoped to step f because both handlers needed companyId anyway.
designDecisions.goalUpdateProgressNormalization: goal.updated payload includes progress: number (0..1). The DB column progressPct is 0..100 integer per the Phase 3 M15 convention (grep confirms `progressPct = (completed projects / total projects) * 100` in goals.ts). The handler re-reads the goal row after the write and divides by 100 for the payload. Rationale: renderer progress bars consume a ratio consistently across goal/project/ticket events; keeping the normalized value in the payload avoids a follow-up goals.get round-trip for every update listener.
designDecisions.patchedKeysCallerIntent: tickets.update / projects.update / goals.update handlers compute patchedKeys from the REQUEST fields (not post-write row state). Tracks caller intent, not row state. Empty-patch still emits (matches companies.update convention) so optimistic renderer paths can reconcile timestamps. goals.update includes a 'progress' slot on patchedKeys only when progressPct was explicitly patched — linked-project status changes emit via projects.update/delete, which already call goalsRepo.recalcProgress internally and emit their own project.* events.
designDecisions.hookWideningPattern: Each of the 3 widened hooks extends its existing if/else-chain filter with the new event-type literals. No restructuring — purely additive string literals added to the || chain. Preserves the pre-existing task.delegated / plan.* / plan.approved subscriptions so M32 planner writes continue to invalidate alongside the new direct-lifecycle events.
designDecisions.testFileConsolidation: Created ONE focused test file (invariant-11-emit-handlers.test.ts) rather than three per-namespace files. Rationale: the 14 emits share one design contract (invariant #11 + HUMAN_USER_ID + try/catch swallow + bus-unwired-tolerance) and a single test file with 23 tests covering all 14 emits is more coherent than three 8-test files with shared scaffolding. Mirrors the M-E process-safeguards model (one check-claim-evidence.test.ts covers the whole engine).
designDecisions.noIpcContractChange: No IPC request/response types changed. projectsLinkTicket/UnlinkTicket added a server-side not-found validation throw, which is a breaking change ONLY for callers previously passing phantom projectIds — and there are none in the codebase (grep-verified). Preload bridge untouched; renderer callers' TypeScript signatures unchanged.

stepsShipped.safeguardProof: Seventh M-C proof at scale of the M-E safeguards. S2 conformance gate expected GREEN on a4dc24e (no new IPC channels, no allowlist movement — the additive emit surface is not a claim-evidence gate subject). S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff. S4 verifiedBy populated on this paired ledger entry per contract (this exact block). M-E acceptance #ciGate continues to validate — six additive commits (a/b/c/d/e/f) + one hardening commit (d-hardening) across the full M-C run, every one clean through the safeguards.
stepsShipped.nextStep: M-C FULLY COMPLETE including step f completeness hardening. Head-of-queue advances to M-D (UI Backfill). M-D ships the renderer surface for Cluster A multi-company CRUD (WorkspaceSwitcher + CreateCompanyDialog + CompanySettings panel wired to companies.create/update/archive/delete) + Cluster B org chart (indented-list tree UI + drag-to-rearrange wired to orgchart.get + employees.promote + employees.setManager) + E2E specs for user-facing surface. With step f landed, every M-D UI component can rely on the full bus-event surface for cache invalidation — the renderer hooks are already widened to subscribe to the 14 new event types, so any ticket/project/goal mutation from the UI will invalidate its query without onSuccess coupling. notes: M-C is the first sprint to run UNDER the live M-E safeguards. The CI conformance gate runs on every M-C commit; the pre-commit hook fast-paths CLAUDE.md edits; the DoD template governs M-C exit; Loki verifiedBy is required on every shipped sub-milestone. The safeguards' first proof is the M-E atomic commit (e4f681a); M-C provides the second proof at scale.

Restoration order WITHIN M-C: (a) Migration 0013 first (Cluster B foundation). (b) companies.create + ensureSystemAgent/Copilot bootstrap (unblocks future multi-company demos). (c) Org-chart repo + orgchart.get IPC. (d) employees.promote + setManager (depends on org-chart repo). (e) companies.update + delete. Each step is its own atomic commit + paired ledger; allowlist entry deleted in the same atomic.

Cherry-pick the worktree-phase-2-the-org branch's commits as REFERENCE not source per CONTRIBUTING.md §Branch Policy. Each cherry-picked commit message MUST cite source SHA + audit row + paired test (per the cherry-pick contract).

If a cherry-pick conflicts non-trivially (>20 lines OR involves a renamed module), abandon the cherry-pick and re-implement from scratch with the branch as a reference, not a source. Document the abandonment in the audit doc.

Each restore commit triggers the conformance check, which removes the corresponding allowlist entry. By M-C exit, scripts/check-claim-evidence.allowlist.json should contain only the 3 MCP naming-drift entries (those land in M-F as docs-only IPC table renames).
Do NOT start M-D (UI backfill) before M-C ships. M-D depends on the IPC + repo surface M-C delivers.
The audit doc §20.2 + §20.3 + §20.4 + §20.5 + §20.6 are authoritative scoping surfaces — do not invent rows mid-flight without invoking the change-control mini-gate (plan §14.3).
